question.text: What did RBI's September 2020 guidelines require banks to establish regarding compliance?

answer.text: An independent corporate compliance function headed by a Chief Compliance Officer (CCO) appointed for a minimum of three years in the rank of GM or not below two levels of CEO rank
A compliance committee with external independent directors only
A compliance audit by external auditors every quarter
A dedicated compliance technology system approved by RBI

explanation.text: **The Logic:** RBI in September 2020 issued guidelines requiring banks to set up an independent corporate compliance function headed by a designated CCO selected through appropriate 'fit and proper' criteria. The CCO should be appointed for a minimum fixed period of 3 years, in the rank of GM or not below two levels of CEO rank. **The Trap:** The requirement is for a CCO position (not just a committee or audit), with specific tenure and rank requirements. The CCO may only be transferred/removed before tenure completion with explicit prior board approval. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The minimum 3-year tenure and board approval for early removal protect CCO independence — similar in spirit to the CO independence provisions in SEBI regulations.
